artifact_goal: Board memo for quarterly shareholder meeting

sources.title: Q1 2026 Financial Export
sources.notes: Primary source for Q1 actuals, including ARR, revenue, gross margin, NRR, churn, cash, burn multiple, and enterprise ARR.

sources.title: CFO Forecast Notes
sources.notes: Primary for forecast assumptions and risk framing, secondary for actuals pending Finance reconciliation.

sources.title: Prior Board Deck Summary
sources.notes: Useful for comparing January plan to Q1 actuals. Not a source for current actuals.

sources.title: Revenue Ops Meeting Transcript
sources.notes: Secondary source for pipeline coverage, enterprise expansion risk, Atlas timing, and CRM data authority.

sources.title: Customer Pipeline Export
sources.notes: Primary source for opportunity stage, amount, close quarter, forecast category, and owner.

facts.statement: Finance close export reports Q1 2026 ARR of $48.2M.
Finance close export reports Q1 2026 revenue of $11.8M.
Finance close export reports Q1 2026 gross margin of 74%.
Finance close export reports Q1 2026 net revenue retention of 118% and logo churn of 3.8%.
Finance close export reports Q1 2026 cash balance of $28.4M and burn multiple of 1.7x.
Finance close export reports Q1 2026 enterprise ARR of $29.1M.
The January board deck is superseded by Q1 close and should not be used as the source of current actuals.
CRM pipeline data was refreshed on 2026-04-09 and should be treated as the system of record for opportunity stage and amount.
Atlas Manufacturing is a $1.4M Q2 enterprise opportunity in Legal Review and Commit forecast category.

assumptions.statement: Enterprise expansion is expected to contribute 55% of Q2 new ARR.
Gross margin is expected to improve to 76% by Q3 if services utilization holds.
Burn multiple should remain below 1.8x through Q3.
The forecast assumes no material churn increase in May or June.

conflicts.description: Board ARR is $48.2M in the Finance close export but $49.1M in CFO planning notes. CFO notes explicitly say Finance must reconcile before publishing.
conflicts.resolution: Do not present a final reconciled ARR figure until Finance resolves the conflict. Memo may note ARR reconciliation is pending and cite both values only if necessary.

conflicts.description: Q2 pipeline coverage is 3.4x in CFO notes but around 3.3x in the RevOps transcript. RevOps action item says the figure must be confirmed.
conflicts.resolution: Use a range for Q2 pipeline coverage rather than a single point estimate.

conflicts.description: Enterprise expansion is described as the main upside driver, but RevOps and CFO comments say it should not be presented as guaranteed while legal and renewal blockers remain.
conflicts.resolution: Frame enterprise expansion as both the upside case and a concentration/renewal risk.

open_questions.question: Should the board memo use $48.2M ARR from Finance close or $49.1M ARR from CFO planning notes?
Should Q2 pipeline coverage be reported as 3.3x, 3.4x, or a range?
Should enterprise expansion concentration be framed as upside, risk, or both?
Has Legal updated the two enterprise renewal blockers after the April 19 action item deadline?
